_comment: Registry of every stateful instrument in the lab and its DECLARED relationship to time. lag_report.py reads this to tell deliberate freezes apart from silent drift. remediate.py reads the 'remediation' block to tell drift that a MACHINE can fix (mode=command) from drift that only a JUDGING MIND can fix (mode=judgment). Add a row here whenever you build a new stateful instrument; an unregistered store is, by policy, a drift waiting to be discovered late. Every 'live' or 'sampled' instrument MUST declare a remediation mode, or remediate.py treats the missing mode as its own drift.

remediation.reason: frozen on purpose; nothing to remediate unless it MOVES

instruments.reason: General session mirror deliberately frozen 2026-05-02 (persistence-layer README status note); superseded by narrower instruments. Guarded by context_card --check-stale since 2026-06-15.
Superseded by pull_graph_v1 on 2026-05-30; v0 retained as history, not maintained.

remediation.reason: Catching up is purely mechanical: recompute the citation graph over the current corpus and append a snapshot. No judgment is involved, so this is exactly the chore that should fire without me. remediate.py runs this and re-checks lag afterward.

instruments.reason: Caught up each weekly lab session (snapshot 5 -> front 116 on 2026-06-29). NOTE 2026-06-29: the IMPLEMENTATION-NOTES 'one snapshot per newly-landed essay / continuous-read' cadence is a fiction in a weekly lab — nothing runs the snapshot between sessions, so it WILL drift to ~essays-landed-per-week between checks. So 'live' here operationally means 'lag 0 at each lab session', and max_lag is set to one week's realistic essay yield (8). NOTE 2026-07-06: the mechanical catch-up is now declared as remediation.mode=command, so remediate.py can close the loop; the manual step becomes running remediate.py --apply, not remembering the exact snapshot invocation.

remediation.note: Read the 5 frozen sample essays (044, 050, 056, 062, 068) and score welcome-temperature by hand. This is NOT automatable: the whole point of the reread is a fresh judgment about how the corpus greets a returning reader. A machine 'backfill' here would be a lie — a timestamp with no reading behind it. remediate.py must escalate, never auto-run.
remediation.escalation: Do the reread this session, then append a scored reading to hospitality_history.json.

instruments.reason: Weekly reread of a frozen 5-essay sample. Recency-based, not frontier-based: it should be READ on cadence, not extended.
